Musk says Tesla’s FSD 14.3 set for broader release by week’s end (TSLA:NASDAQ). Elon Musk announced that Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) version 14.3 is currently in employee beta testing. He anticipates a wider rollout of the software to the public by the end of the current week. Tesla (TSLA) shares are being closely watched following this development.

NTSB Finds Ford's BlueCruise Driver Assistance System At Fault For Two Fatal Collisions. The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) has attributed two fatal collisions in early 2024 to Ford's BlueCruise driver assistance system, citing its failure to stop for stationary vehicles and detect driver disengagement. The NTSB is calling for stronger safety standards, better oversight, and improvements to crash data recording and driver monitoring systems for partial automation technologies. This comes as Ford plans to offer "eyes-off" technology by 2028 and amid an ongoing NHTSA investigation into Tesla's Full Self-Driving system.

Why UBER Stock At $70 Is A Steal. Uber Technologies (UBER) is presented as a compelling investment at $70, having transitioned from a high-burn startup to a profitable cash-flow engine. The company benefits from strong advertising revenue growth, massive user scale, a successful membership program (Uber One), and increasing free cash flow driven by operating leverage. However, the article also addresses significant risks, including potential regulatory changes impacting driver classification and the emerging threat of robotaxi services from competitors like Tesla and Waymo.

TD (TD) offers $1,000 auto‑callable, contingent‑coupon notes linked to AVGO/META/NVDA/TSLA. The Toronto-Dominion Bank is offering $1,000 equity-linked, auto-callable notes with contingent coupons tied to the performance of Broadcom (AVGO), Meta (META), NVIDIA (NVDA), and Tesla (TSLA). These notes feature a minimum 24.85% annual contingent coupon rate, an April 10, 2026 pricing date, an April 15, 2026 issue date, and an April 13, 2029 maturity date. Payments and automatic calls are contingent on the lowest performing underlying stock, exposing investors to significant downside risk if any one of the stocks performs poorly.

Tesla French car registrations triple in March. Tesla's new car registrations in March more than tripled in France and doubled in Denmark, signaling a recovery in the EV giant's sales in Europe. After a difficult year marred by increasing competition and lack of new models, the introduction of cheaper Model Y and Model 3 versions has led to a return to growth. Registrations in France reached 9,569 units, a 203.10% increase year-over-year, while Denmark saw a 144% rise to 1,447 units.

GraniteShares Announces Weekly Distributions for its Autocallable ETFs: ANV and TLA. GraniteShares announced the weekly distributions for its Autocallable ETFs, ANV (GraniteShares Autocallable NVDA ETF) and TLA (GraniteShares Autocallable TSLA ETF). The announcement details the distribution per share, distribution rate, 30-day SEC yield, ex-date, record date, and payment date for both ETFs. The company also provides important disclaimers regarding distribution guarantees, performance, and investment risks associated with these funds.

Tesla (TESLA) and SpaceX Plan to Build a New Chip Factory in Texas. Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) and SpaceX are planning to build two advanced chip factories in Austin, Texas, as part of a "Terafab" project. This initiative aims to address future chip demand for Tesla's vehicles and Optimus robots, as well as AI data centers in space, given that current global supply only meets a small fraction of their projected needs. The Terafab venture is a collaboration involving SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI, with no completion date announced.

Tesla named on Iran’s 18-company target list threatening Gulf operations. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has included Tesla on a list of 18 US companies whose Middle East operations are threatened with attack in retaliation for military leader killings, setting an explicit deadline of April 1. Tesla's significant physical presence in the Gulf, including showrooms and Supercharger stations, makes it a unique target compared to other tech and defense companies primarily operating data centers. The threat comes as Tesla has been actively managing regional instability, including offering free Supercharging, and its inclusion on the list is notable given its lack of defense contracts.

TGT: BOF: Revenue doubled, capacity expanded, and major new orders set the stage for strong growth. BranchOut Food Inc. (BOF) has reported a significant increase in revenue, which more than doubled to $14.3 million, alongside an adjusted gross margin of 27.8%. The company is expanding its production capacity with a fourth line and has secured major new orders from Sam's Club and Target, signaling strong future growth. Annual revenue is projected to exceed $20 million, supported by diversified customer relationships.

Ecommerce earnings recap: What you missed from Lovesac, Shoe Carnival and more. This article provides an ecommerce earnings recap for several retailers, including Lovesac and Shoe Carnival, highlighting their performance in the latest fiscal quarters. Lovesac experienced a 2.7% rise in net sales, with internet sales outpacing showroom sales, while Shoe Carnival saw a 3.4% decline in net sales, though its Shoe Station brand showed promising online growth. Other major retailers like Chewy, Alibaba, Amazon, Costco, Kroger, Home Depot, Target, and Walmart also reported their recent financial results, with many showing strong digital sales growth despite various market challenges.

Target Corp. stock rises Tuesday, still underperforms market. Shares of Target Corp. (TGT) increased by 2.04% to $121.20 on Tuesday, contributing to an overall positive trading session for the stock market. Despite this rise, Target Corp. still underperformed the broader market, which saw the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average climb significantly. The company's stock closed 3.81% below its 52-week high of $126.00.
SBUX: Starbucks Surges Over 2.7% Amid Governance Shifts, Loyalty Program Overhaul, and Rallying Investor Sentiment. Starbucks (SBUX) shares surged over 2.7% following corporate updates including governance shifts and a revamped loyalty program, pushing the stock above its 200-day moving average. Despite mixed investor sentiment regarding the loyalty program and labor relations, analysts have adjusted their outlooks, with Guggenheim raising its target to $95. The article also details options trading opportunities for aggressive bulls looking to capitalize on potential price movements ahead of the April 10 expiration.

[PX14A6G] STARBUCKS CORP SEC Filing. This SEC filing (Form PX14A6G) by Starbucks Corporation details an exempt solicitation by the Comptroller of the City of New York on behalf of several New York City pension funds. The solicitation urges Starbucks shareholders to vote against the re-election of Board Directors Jørgen Vig Knudstorp and Beth Ford due to ongoing labor issues and a rollback of independent board oversight since 2021. The filing includes texts of social media posts by the NYC Comptroller disseminating this recommendation.
DIS: Raymond James upgrades Disney stock rating on valuation opportunity. Raymond James has upgraded Walt Disney (NYSE:DIS) stock rating from Market Perform to Outperform, setting a price target of $115.00, citing an attractive valuation opportunity amidst current macro and international visitation headwinds. Despite potential challenges in its Experiences segment and macro risks, the firm views the risk-reward profile as attractive and the stock as historically cheap. This upgrade aligns with InvestingPro data suggesting Disney is undervalued, highlighting a low P/E ratio relative to near-term earnings growth.

Brookstone Capital Management Has $14.39 Million Stock Position in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Brookstone Capital Management increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) by 8.1% in Q4, now holding 126,456 shares valued at $14.39 million. This comes as institutional investors collectively own about 65.7% of the stock. Despite a Q1 revenue beat, significant growth in streaming operating income, and analyst support, the company faces near-term PR and execution risks due to setbacks in AI initiatives like the collapsed Sora/OpenAI collaboration and an AI Olaf malfunction.

General Mills Embraces the Rainbow: A Natural Makeover for Lucky Charms and Trix. General Mills is committing to remove all certified synthetic dyes from its U.S. cereal portfolio by summer 2026, spearheading this change with new Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy and Tropical Trix with Disney's Moana. This initiative is a response to consumer demand, regulatory pressures, and advancements in food science, particularly in stable natural pigments. The company aims for its entire U.S. retail portfolio to be free of synthetic dyes by the end of 2027, learning from a previous attempt in 2016 to introduce natural Trix.

Mickey & Friends Saddle Up For A Reimagining Of Disney Songs With A Spectacular Line Up Of Country Music Stars. Disney has announced an all-new country EP titled "Main Street Country," set to release on April 3, featuring award-winning artists who reimagine classic Disney songs with a country twist. The EP includes performances by Kane and Katelyn Brown, Restless Road, Dasha, Maddie & Tae, and BRELAND, covering iconic tracks from films like "Beauty and the Beast," "Hannah Montana: The Movie," and "Hercules." This initiative follows the success of a pop-punk Disney cover album and continues the Mickey & Friends music series, blending nostalgia with modern country storytelling.

Lululemon convinces US judge to overturn Nike shoe patent verdict. A federal judge in Manhattan has overturned a jury's verdict that Lululemon infringed a Nike shoe patent, invalidating the patent and reversing a $355,450 award against Lululemon. Nike had sued Lululemon in 2023, claiming its shoes infringed two patents related to manufacturing techniques, but the judge ruled the asserted patent was invalid due to obviousness. A separate patent infringement case by Nike against Lululemon concerning its Mirror Home Gym is still ongoing.

Earnings Flash (NKE) NIKE, Inc. Reports Q3 Revenue $11.28B, vs. FactSet Est of $11.23B. NIKE, Inc. reported its Q3 revenue at $11.28 billion, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $11.23 billion. Despite beating revenue expectations, several analysts have adjusted their ratings and price targets for Nike, citing a slower than planned pace of recovery and delayed sales. The stock has plummeted to its lowest in a decade, influenced by broader market concerns and hopes for an end to the Iran war.

NIKE, Inc. Reports Fiscal 2026 Third Quarter Results. NIKE, Inc. reported its fiscal 2026 third-quarter results, with revenues flat at $11.3 billion on a reported basis and down 3% currency-neutral, and diluted earnings per share at $0.35, a 35% decrease. The company highlighted actions taken to improve business health, with efforts to drive momentum in prioritized areas despite declines in EMEA and Greater China. Despite challenges, management expressed confidence in profitable long-term growth and its track record of shareholder returns, including consistent dividend payouts.

McCormick, Allbirds And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Tuesday. This article highlights several stocks to watch, including McCormick & Company Inc. and Allbirds Inc., ahead of Tuesday's market open. McCormick is expected to report its quarterly earnings, while Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc. announced mixed fourth-quarter results and progress on its new SpaceShip. Allbirds shares surged after the company announced an agreement to sell most of its intellectual property, and TD Synnex Corp. and Nike Inc. are also expected to release earnings.

Profit drops at NIKE (NYSE: NKE) despite flat Q3 2026 sales. NIKE, Inc. reported mixed fiscal Q3 2026 results with revenue remaining flat at $11.3 billion but a significant 35% drop in net income to $520 million and diluted earnings per share to $0.35. This decline was primarily attributed to lower gross margins, driven by higher North America tariffs, and an increased effective tax rate. The company also saw uneven performance across brands and channels, with wholesale revenue rising but NIKE Direct and Converse revenues falling.

Nike Delivers Q3 Earnings Beat As Turnaround Progresses, Shares Slide. Nike Inc. reported better-than-expected third-quarter fiscal 2026 results with revenues of $11.28 billion and earnings of 35 cents per share. Despite the earnings beat and flat year-over-year revenue growth, Nike's shares slid in after-hours trading. The company emphasized ongoing efforts to improve business health and progress in its turnaround strategy.
